meaning: n.扣子,钩,紧握,抱住v.扣紧,紧握,搂抱,密切合作
examples: She had sat there twiddling nervously with the clasp of her handbag.
He took her hand in his firm warm clasp.
We needed to order a whole new motherboard, if only for the clasp attached to it.
The MoD has also announced a Bomber Command clasp, for bravery and service during the war.
